current_conditions.site_name: SOQUE RIVER AT GA 197, NEAR CLARKESVILLE, GA

light_pressure_read.read: High pressure and low cloud cover make this fishable but technical.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.
fly_reasoning.zebra_midge: Small and natural for clear, pressured, technical conditions.
fly_reasoning.pheasant_tail: A reliable natural nymph when trout are feeding but cautious.

caution_notes: Bright/high-pressure conditions may make fish cover-oriented and less forgiving.

generated_text.quick_read: The Soque River currently offers fair trout fishing with stable flow and moderate gage height. Recent stocking enhances trout activity despite unavailable water temperature data.
generated_text.best_window: The best fishing window is day 1 morning due to low winds, high humidity, minimal sky cover, and nearly no rain, creating stable conditions for trout to feed near shade and structure.
generated_text.current_conditions: Flow is 64 cfs, gage height is 3.55 ft, water temperature is unavailable, and turbidity data is not available.
generated_text.weather_setup: Currently, the sky cover averages 21% with high pressure at 30.11 inHg and an east wind averaging 2.2 mph. Rain probability is low with no expected rainfall.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout are likely to hold in deeper runs, shade, undercut banks, and near structure due to bright, high-pressure conditions. Use smaller, natural flies and attractors like egg pattern, squirmy worm, and duracell for careful, less aggressive presentations. Exercise caution as trout may be cover-oriented and less forgiving in these conditions.
